task_completion.description: Verify that the TelcoChurn dataset is loaded into JASP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the JASP application window displaying the 'TelcoChurn' data either in the spreadsheet view or in the variable list of an analysis panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that Cox Proportional Hazards regression is configured with the correct variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the JASP analysis input panel. 'tenure' should be in the 'Time' or 'Survival Time' box, 'Churn' in the 'Event' box (with 'Yes' defined as the event), and 'Contract', 'PaymentMethod', and 'MonthlyCharges' in the 'Covariates' box.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Proportional Hazards assumption test and a Forest plot are generated in the results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the JASP results panel on the right. There should be a table for the 'Proportional Hazards Test' (or similar wording) and a visual 'Forest Plot' displayed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the JASP project is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the JASP window title bar for 'ChurnAnalysis.jasp', or look for a file manager/save dialog showing 'ChurnAnalysis.jasp' saved in the /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the text report file is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) open with the filename 'churn_risk_report.txt', or a terminal command showing the creation of this file in /home/ga/Documents/JASP/.

task_completion.description: Verify the text report contains the correct Hazard Ratio, p-value, and interpretation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of 'churn_risk_report.txt'. It must contain a Hazard Ratio (Exp(B)) for 'Contract: Two year' between 0.0 and 0.15 (specifically around 0.018), a p-value for the Global Proportional Hazards Test that is < 0.05, and a written statement concluding that the proportional hazards assumption was violated/not met.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the JASP GUI to perform the analysis rather than a Python/R script in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the JASP graphical user interface being actively used to configure the Cox regression and view results.

integrity.description: Verify the values typed into the text report match the actual results generated in the JASP GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Cross-reference the Hazard Ratio and p-value visible in the JASP results panel with the numbers typed into the text report. They should match, indicating the agent didn't just guess or hardcode the expected answer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually extracted the insights rather than copying a pre-existing solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the text report is being actively written or pasted from the JASP results, rather than being downloaded or copied from an external script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct Hazard Ratio (Exp(B)) for a 'Two year' contract relative to month-to-month is approximately 0.018 (acceptable range is 0.0 to 0.15). The Global Proportional Hazards Test yields a p-value < 0.05 (specifically < 2e-16). Because the p-value is significant (< 0.05), the correct interpretation is that the proportional hazards assumption is VIOLATED (or not met).
